c74object.name: tap.svf~
c74object.description: The tap.svf~ object is a state-variable filter built on Andy Simper's trapezoidal-integration (zero-delay-feedback) SVF — the design behind the Cytomic filters in Ableton Live. The prewarped tuning is exact into the top octaves at any sample rate, and the filter stays stable under per-sample cutoff modulation (a signal in the right inlet). Beyond the discrete responses (lowpass, highpass, bandpass, notch, peak, allpass) there is a morph type that sweeps continuously around lowpass, bandpass, highpass, and notch — like the Morph filter in Live's Auto Filter — and parametric-EQ types (bell, low shelf, high shelf) with a boost/cut gain for building equalizers. The order attribute selects 2nd-, 4th-, or 8th-order responses (12/24/48 dB per octave) as a cascade with the Butterworth Q spread, so resonance 0 is maximally flat at every order and raising the resonance (0 to 1) sharpens a single clean peak. Two circuits are available: clean is the pure linear filter; driven adds an input drive into a tanh limiter on each section's band node, runs oversampled, and self-oscillates at the cutoff when resonance reaches 1 (give it a ping to start — a perfectly silent filter is a fixed point). All parameters glide smoothly. Single-channel: wrap in mc. for multichannel.

method.name: float
method.description: In the right inlet, sets the cutoff frequency in Hz.

method.name: q
method.description: Set the resonance attribute from a Q value (the Q of the resonant section; the Butterworth base Q of the current order is the minimum). Without an argument, posts the current equivalent Q to the Max console.

method.name: clear
method.description: Reset the filter state (this also stops self-oscillation).

attribute.name: type
attribute.description: Filter response: lowpass, highpass, bandpass, notch, peak, allpass, morph (continuous sweep positioned by the morph attribute), or the parametric-EQ types bell, lowshelf, and highshelf (boost/cut set by the gain attribute; the EQ types always run 2nd-order).

attribute.name: frequency
attribute.description: Cutoff / center frequency in Hz (1-20000). A signal in the right inlet overrides this with per-sample resolution.

attribute.name: resonance
attribute.description: Resonance (0-1). 0 is maximally flat (Butterworth); rising values sharpen a single resonant peak. At 1 the clean circuit reaches a very high finite Q; the driven circuit self-oscillates at the cutoff. Use the q message to set this from a Q value.

attribute.name: morph
attribute.description: Position around the response circle when type is morph: 0 = lowpass, 0.25 = bandpass, 0.5 = highpass, 0.75 = notch, 1 wraps back to lowpass. The corners are identical to the discrete types.

attribute.name: gain
attribute.description: Boost/cut in dB (-24..24) for the parametric-EQ types (bell, lowshelf, highshelf); ignored by the other types.

attribute.name: drive
attribute.description: Input drive in dB (-24..24) into the driven circuit's saturation; ignored by the clean circuit.

attribute.name: circuit
attribute.description: clean: the pure linear SVF (transparent, cheap, no oversampling). driven: adds drive into a tanh limiter on each section's band node — saturation color, bounded resonance, true self-oscillation at resonance 1 — and runs oversampled.

attribute.name: order
attribute.description: Filter order: 2, 4, or 8 (12/24/48 dB per octave), as a cascade with the Butterworth Q spread — resonance 0 stays maximally flat at every order. The EQ types always run 2nd-order.

attribute.name: oversample
attribute.description: Internal oversampling factor for the driven circuit (1, 2, or 4; default 2). Reduces aliasing from the saturation. The clean circuit is linear and always runs 1x.

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Anti-zipper interpolation time in milliseconds applied to parameter changes.
